title: Request: Another option when SQL fails to connect
author: LaurenceMillward
body_snippet:
Hi,

After playing around with the SQL settings i've managed to accidentally overwrite my whole database with a old connections file.

mRemoteNG failed to connect to the database so gave me the option of either using a connections file, or quitting the program. After choosing a connections file that I haven't used in a few months just so i can actually edit the SQL settings it it loaded into the application, I changed the SQL settings and BOOM, the whole database was overridden.

Could the...

iterations.description: Issue has no body or description explaining what 'another option when SQL fails to connect' means - unclear what behavior is requested or what the current problem is

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

notes:
AI triage: Issue has no body or description explaining what 'another option when SQL fails to connect' means - unclear what behavior is requested or what the current problem is
Approach: Need reporter to clarify: what option they want (fallback to local XML, retry prompt, silent continue, etc.) and what currently happens when SQL connection fails
